message:
chore(beads,tests,ci): update issue tracker, expand continuity E2E test, and harden conformance runner

- Update beads issue statuses reflecting concurrent agent progress
- Add new assertions to e2e_continuity.c for stronger verification
- Improve run_conformance.sh with better error handling and reporting

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1md.15","title":"Capture vertical and continuity fixture families (HFT microburst, automotive watchdog, crash/restart replay)","description":"Background:
Cross-vertical and restart continuity promises need first-class fixture coverage from the Rust reference baseline.

Subtasks:
- Capture HFT burst/overload fixtures with deterministic backpressure outcomes.
- Capture automotive deadline/watchdog/degraded-mode fixtures.
- Capture persisted trace/snapshot crash-restart continuity fixtures.
- Build vertical and continuity fixtures as extensions of the core fixture baseline (bd-1md.13) to preserve shared-kernel semantic identity.
- Map each scenario to profile parity and release evidence requirements.
- Define matching e2e scenario scripts and structured log/manifest obligations for these vertical families.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Vertical and continuity fixtures are reproducible and canonicalized.
- Expected semantic digests and failure outcomes are explicit.
- Fixture families plug directly into profile gates and release verification.
- Vertical fixtures preserve explicit linkage to shared core fixture semantics.
- Vertical e2e runs produce complete, audit-ready artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:02.535740899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:37:19.510886625Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:37:19.510865004Z","close_reason":"Completed vertical/continuity fixture-family contract, schema, and matrix  {"id":"bd-1md.16","title":"Implement e2e script family for core lifecycle + codec/parity scenarios","description":"Background:
Core e2e scripts must validate lifecycle semantics, cancellation/close behavior, timer/channel interactions, and codec equivalence with deterministic manifests.

Subtasks:
- Implement scripts for region/task/obligation lifecycle, cancellation propagation, quiescence close paths, timer progression, and channel reserve/send/abort flows.
- Add JSON/BIN codec equivalence runs and semantic-digest identity checks across supported profile lanes.
- Emit structured manifests with run_id, seed, scenario IDs, profile/codec, compiler target, first-failure pointers, and rerun commands.
- Map each e2e lane to unit/invariant obligations from `bd-1md.12` so script coverage and unit coverage remain synchronized.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Core/codec e2e suite is deterministic and CI-ready across repeated runs.
- Artifacts include complete, schema-valid logs/manifests suitable for triage and audit.
- Every e2e lane has explicit linkage to corresponding unit/invariant obligations and parity fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:11:43.969303989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:48:34.519609267Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:48:34.519586664Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","core","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1md.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:11:43.969303989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:21:44.701878528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:11:44.844857420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:07:50.260598849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:11:44.558138770Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:12:59.375927109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:29:56.962214582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.16","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:16:33.565104263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.16","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:16:33.655093485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.16","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:08:33.314786494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1md.17","title":"Implement e2e script family for robustness boundaries and fault-injection scenarios","description":"Background:
Robustness e2e scripts must stress allocator/resource boundaries, malformed-input edges, and injected fault paths with deterministic outcomes.

Subtasks:
- Implement scripts for exhaustion boundaries, endian/unaligned safety checks, stale-handle misuse, and controlled fault-injection modes.
- Capture deterministic outcomes, failure-atomicity evidence, and containment-path behavior in structured manifests.
- Emit minimized first-failure summaries with precise rerun hints and artifact pointers.
- Link robustness e2e lanes to corresponding unit/invariant obligations and forbidden-behavior rows.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Robustness e2e suite reproduces deterministic outcomes across reruns and environments.
- Logs/manifests are complete, schema-valid, and actionable for debugging.
- Evidence demonstrates no partial-mutation corruption on robustness failure paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:11:44.071807833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:54:59.565144144Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:54:59.565119007Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","robustness","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1md.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:11:44.071807833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:11:44.937566211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:07:50.354220915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:11:44.657690151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:12:23.121821061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:12:59.475391768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:29:57.420643182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.17","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:16:33.744589402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.17","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:16:33.839367835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1md.19","title":"Create API misuse catalog and fixtureize invalid-usage behavior","description":"Background:
Plan section 7.3.4 introduces a public API misuse catalog to compensate for missing Rust compile-time misuse rejection.

Subtasks:
- Author `docs/API_MISUSE_CATALOG.md` with concrete invalid-usage examples and expected outcomes.
- Convert misuse cases into both unit/invariant tests and conformance fixtures across codecs/profiles where applicable.
- Add dedicated misuse e2e scenarios with structured logs/manifests and first-failure rerun hints.
- Add CI checks ensuring misuse tests/fixtures remain stable and deterministic.
- Link catalog cases to public API docs and error-code families.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Misuse catalog is complete for public kernel API surface.
- Invalid-use behavior is regression-tested via unit/invariant/e2e/fixture artifacts.
- API changes that alter misuse behavior require explicit review and documented rationale.
- Misuse evidence is machine-traceable in parity reports and test manifests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:11.032381623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:45:24.657042087Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:45:24.657024183Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","conformance","fixtures","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:11.032381623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:58.333060734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:21:44.805387804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:12:23.646832336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:58.214173119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:22:26.422068225Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T02:22:26.516518724Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-296.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:58.451481103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:57.916684799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:58.006923600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.19","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:58.099300026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1md.2","title":"Implement C conformance runner and machine-readable parity reports","description":"Background:
Conformance runner is the semantic referee between Rust and C; provenance mismatch must fail hard.

Subtasks:
- Implement deterministic runner for fixture replay against C runtime.
- Compare outputs/digests across JSON and binary codec modes.
- Enforce fixture provenance validation (`rust_baseline_commit`, toolchain hash, schema versions) with hard-fail on mismatch.
- Emit machine-readable parity reports including baseline/provenance metadata and diff artifacts.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Runner deterministically evaluates all in-scope fixture families.
- Provenance mismatch is rejected automatically (no silent fixture mixing).
- Parity reports are machine-readable and directly consumable by CI gates.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:14.723664218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:23:43.945404201Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:23:43.945386077Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","runner","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1md.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:14.723664218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:15.356773749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:11:03.495036076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:15.442539810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1md.20","title":"Implement shared e2e harness contract for scenario packs, manifests, and rerun UX","description":"Background:
E2E family beads need a shared harness layer so scripts stay consistent, diagnosable, and maintainable across core/robustness/vertical packs.

Subtasks:
- Define shared CLI/env contract (seed, profile, codec, scenario-pack, resource-class, run-id).
- Implement reusable helpers for manifest emission, first-failure summaries, rerun command generation, and artifact path layout.
- Integrate harness with structured log schema and conformance-runner hooks.
- Add harness-level smoke tests validating deterministic outputs and error handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- All e2e family script beads consume the shared harness contract.
- Manifest/log outputs are schema-consistent across script families.
- Operators can rerun failures from emitted command snippets without manual reconstruction.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:29:51.324639352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:37:05.320240641Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:37:05.320222197Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","harness","logging","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1md.20","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:29:51.324639352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.20","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:29:56.075737057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1md.20","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:29:56.492089237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -121,7 +121,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ix8.6","title":"Implement compile-time memory-layout and budget-delta reporting per target","description":"Background:
Plan calls for per-target visibility into memory layout and budget drift to protect constrained deployments.

Subtasks:
- Generate compile-time reports for key structs and allocator budgets per target.
- Diff reports across target matrix and flag regressions.
- Integrate report artifacts into CI outputs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Reports are reproducible and machine-comparable.
- Budget deltas are surfaced before deployment regressions.

 {"id":"bd-ix8.7","title":"Implement endian and unaligned-access hardening suite for binary transport paths","description":"Background:
Binary codec correctness depends on explicit handling of endian and alignment differences.

Subtasks:
- Add compile/runtime checks for endian assumptions.
- Add tests and fixtures for unaligned access edge cases.
- Verify binary decode behavior on target matrix and emulated runs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No undefined behavior from alignment/endian assumptions.
- Edge fixtures pass consistently across matrix targets.
- Failures produce actionable diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:19.494070205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:42:58.547008391Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:42:58.546987291Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","codec","idea-20","portability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-296.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:45.813516890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:20.777679648Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:19.494070205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:20.674659933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:31.248275510Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ix8.8","title":"Implement Phase 2.5 walking skeleton end-to-end lifecycle path","description":"Background:
Plan phase 2.5 adds a mandatory end-to-end smoke path to surface integration mismatches early.

Subtasks:
- Implement minimal region open/close + task create/poll-ready path through real public headers.
- Implement trivial scheduler loop that can drive one no-op task to completion and assert quiescence.
- Add deterministic integration test and run manifest/log output for portability matrix + QEMU targets.
- Keep implementation minimal and explicitly non-final, focused on proving layer wiring.

Acceptance Criteria:
- One real lifecycle path executes correctly across required CI and QEMU targets.
- Integration test artifacts are deterministic and reproducible.
- Phase-3 expansion work uses this skeleton as baseline safety net.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:24:55.241073418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:08:00.037263907Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:08:00.037244581Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","integration","phase-2","smoke"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-296.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:50.892535299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:24:55.241073418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:50.709928758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:50.802461697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:47.691978700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:47.789134130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-j4m","title":"EPIC: Cross-Vertical Profiles (Embedded Router, HFT, Automotive) with Zero Semantic Drift","description":"Background:
This project must serve multiple hard domains without splitting semantics into incompatible forks.

Goal:
Implement profile-specific operational tuning while enforcing semantic digest equivalence across shared fixtures.

Includes:
- embedded resource classes (R1/R2/R3)
- HFT tail/jitter governance
- automotive deadline/watchdog/degraded-mode behavior
- vertical adapters with fallback isomorphism artifacts

Why this serves users:
- domain-specific utility without losing the core architecture
- confidence that profile tuning does not compromise correctness.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.317289058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:37.496783856Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automotive","embedded","hft","phase-8","phase-9","profiles"]}
+{"id":"bd-j4m","title":"EPIC: Cross-Vertical Profiles (Embedded Router, HFT, Automotive) with Zero Semantic Drift","description":"Background:
This project must serve multiple hard domains without splitting semantics into incompatible forks.

Goal:
Implement profile-specific operational tuning while enforcing semantic digest equivalence across shared fixtures.

Includes:
- embedded resource classes (R1/R2/R3)
- HFT tail/jitter governance
- automotive deadline/watchdog/degraded-mode behavior
- vertical adapters with fallback isomorphism artifacts

Why this serves users:
- domain-specific utility without losing the core architecture
- confidence that profile tuning does not compromise correctness.

Cross-cutting quality contract:

- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.317289058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T21:54:44.425627935Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automotive","embedded","hft","phase-8","phase-9","profiles"]}
 {"id":"bd-j4m.1","title":"Implement profile compatibility baseline and cross-profile semantic parity enforcement","description":"Background:
The project must avoid domain forks. Profiles may tune operations but not semantics.

Subtasks:
- Define shared-fixture parity set across core, freestanding, embedded_router, hft, and automotive profiles.
- Implement profile compatibility checks and canonical digest comparisons.
- Document acceptable operational differences versus forbidden semantic differences.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Profile parity runner reports pass/fail with first divergence detail.
- Compatibility rules are explicit and machine-checkable.
- Any semantic drift is blocked by default.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:40.609378813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:34.479362238Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compatibility","parity","profiles"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:53.160983657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:53.258815677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:08.882015154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:41.342385366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:41.240436839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:40.609378813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-j4m.2","title":"Implement embedded_router profile with resource classes and wear-aware diagnostics","description":"Background:
Embedded utility requires low-memory operation and flash-safe diagnostics without losing core semantics.

Subtasks:
- Implement embedded_router defaults for constrained targets.
- Implement resource classes R1, R2, R3 with deterministic reject/backpressure behavior.
- Implement RAM-ring trace mode and wear-aware persistent spill strategy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Router profile passes semantic parity fixtures.
- Resource ceiling failures are deterministic and non-corrupting.
- Diagnostic mode behavior is documented for low-flash devices.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:40.712177293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:34.351677047Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["embedded","profiles","router"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:41.541810409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:41.649164106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.2","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:40.712177293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.2","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:41.438175021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-j4m.3","title":"Implement HFT profile instrumentation, tail/jitter controls, and deterministic overload behavior","description":"Background:
HFT users care most about tail latency, jitter, and deterministic behavior under microburst pressure.

Subtasks:
- Add HFT profile defaults and instrumentation hooks.
- Implement microburst overload handling with deterministic policy outcomes.
- Integrate p99 to p99.99 and jitter reporting with gate hooks.

Acceptance Criteria:
- HFT profile passes semantic parity set and HFT metric gates.
- Overload policy outcomes are deterministic and replayable.
- Benchmark artifacts identify regression root causes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:40.812371231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:39.421180163Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hft","latency","profiles"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:41.838402298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:11:15.520394849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.3","depends_on_id":"bd-296.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:41.878290590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:40.812371231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.3","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:41.736787166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -20,8 +20,8 @@ Options:
   --help
 
 Environment:
-  FAIL_ON_EMPTY_FIXTURES=1      fail when no fixture JSON files are present
-  FAIL_ON_INCOMPLETE_PARITY=1   fail when parity mode has no comparable pairs
+  FAIL_ON_EMPTY_FIXTURES=1      fail when no fixture JSON files are present (default: 1)
+  FAIL_ON_INCOMPLETE_PARITY=1   fail when parity mode has no comparable pairs (default: 1)
   FAIL_ON_MISSING_JQ=1          fail when jq is missing (default: 1)
   CONFORMANCE_CFLAGS="..."      override compile flags for smoke binary
 EOF
@@ -52,8 +52,8 @@ FIXTURES_ROOT="${FIXTURES_ROOT:-$REPO_ROOT/fixtures/rust_reference}"
 REPORT_DIR="${REPORT_DIR:-$REPO_ROOT/tools/ci/artifacts/conformance}"
 RUN_ID=""
 STRICT=0
-FAIL_ON_EMPTY_FIXTURES="${FAIL_ON_EMPTY_FIXTURES:-0}"
-FAIL_ON_INCOMPLETE_PARITY="${FAIL_ON_INCOMPLETE_PARITY:-0}"
+FAIL_ON_EMPTY_FIXTURES="${FAIL_ON_EMPTY_FIXTURES:-1}"
+FAIL_ON_INCOMPLETE_PARITY="${FAIL_ON_INCOMPLETE_PARITY:-1}"
 FAIL_ON_MISSING_JQ="${FAIL_ON_MISSING_JQ:-1}"
 BASELINE_FILE="$REPO_ROOT/docs/rust_baseline_inventory.json"
 SCHEMA_FILE="$REPO_ROOT/schemas/canonical_fixture.schema.json"
@@ -667,6 +667,10 @@ jq -s \
     skip: (map(select(.status == "skip")) | length),
     fixture_records: (map(select(.kind == "fixture")) | length),
     parity_records: (map(select(.kind == "codec_equivalence" or .kind == "profile_parity")) | length),
+    comparable_parity_records: (
+      map(select((.kind == "codec_equivalence" or .kind == "profile_parity") and .status != "skip"))
+      | length
+    ),
     diff_records: (map(select(.status == "fail")) | length),
     fail_by_kind: (
       reduce (map(select(.status == "fail"))[]) as $row
@@ -697,10 +701,11 @@ jq -s \
 fail_count="$(jq -r '.fail' "$SUMMARY_FILE")"
 fixture_count="$(jq -r '.fixture_records' "$SUMMARY_FILE")"
 parity_count="$(jq -r '.parity_records' "$SUMMARY_FILE")"
+comparable_parity_count="$(jq -r '.comparable_parity_records' "$SUMMARY_FILE")"
 diff_count="$(jq -r '.diff_records' "$SUMMARY_FILE")"
 
 echo "[asx] conformance[$MODE]: report=$REPORT_FILE summary=$SUMMARY_FILE" >&2
-echo "[asx] conformance[$MODE]: fixture_records=$fixture_count parity_records=$parity_count fail=$fail_count diff_records=$diff_count" >&2
+echo "[asx] conformance[$MODE]: fixture_records=$fixture_count parity_records=$parity_count comparable_parity_records=$comparable_parity_count fail=$fail_count diff_records=$diff_count" >&2
 
 exit_code=0
 if [[ "$fail_count" -gt 0 ]]; then
@@ -710,7 +715,7 @@ if [[ "$fixture_count" -eq 0 && "$FAIL_ON_EMPTY_FIXTURES" == "1" ]]; then
   echo "[asx] conformance[$MODE]: FAIL (no fixtures and strict empty-fixture policy enabled)" >&2
   exit_code=1
 fi
-if [[ "$MODE" != "conformance" && "$parity_count" -eq 0 && "$FAIL_ON_INCOMPLETE_PARITY" == "1" ]]; then
+if [[ "$MODE" != "conformance" && "$comparable_parity_count" -eq 0 && "$FAIL_ON_INCOMPLETE_PARITY" == "1" ]]; then
   echo "[asx] conformance[$MODE]: FAIL (no comparable parity pairs and strict parity policy enabled)" >&2
   exit_code=1
 fi
